svg.aria-label: Step 1: Disassembling the Top Wooden Plate

g.title: Step 1: Disassembling the Top Wooden Plate
g.desc: This instruction shows the first step of disassembly. Locate the top hourglass-shaped wooden plate, which is secured by two brass screws on opposite ends. Use a screwdriver to remove these two screws. Once the screws are out, lift the top plate straight up. This reveals a black rectangular circuit board with several small square tactile buttons mounted on a matching bottom plate. Two small vertical wooden supports also become visible. Place the removed screws and the top plate into your clean-up box.

g.title: Step 2: Disconnect Jumper and Remove Spacers
g.desc: This disassembly step involves removing several components from a large, controller-shaped wooden base plate. First, locate the jumper wire, which is a flat ribbon of five colored wires with a black plastic connector on the end; pull this connector horizontally away from the pins on the rectangular circuit board to disconnect it. Second, lift the circuit board (identifiable by the eight small tactile buttons on its top surface) straight up. Finally, remove the two 'Large Spacers.' These are long, thin wooden pieces with cross-shaped tabs at each end that fit into slots in the base plate. One is located on the left side and one on the right side of the base; pull both straight up out of their slots.

g.title: Disassembly Complete!
g.desc: If not already done, place each piece on its spot in the clean up area below.

g.title: Step 3: Remove Small Spacers
g.desc: Step 3 shows the disassembly of two small spacers from the large, hourglass-shaped wooden plate. The spacers are small, cross-shaped wooden pieces that are currently slotted into square holes on the top surface of the plate. Identify these by feeling for the small wooden tabs sticking up near the center of the plate. Pull both small spacers straight up and out of their slots, as indicated by the upward-pointing dashed arrows.

g.title: Exploded View of the Controller Assembly
g.desc: This diagram shows how to layer the components of the handheld controller. In the center is a rectangular circuit board with small, raised tactile buttons. A multi-colored ribbon cable attaches to one side of this board. The board is framed by four small wooden support pieces: two long ones for the top and bottom and two shorter ones for the ends. These pieces have cross-shaped tabs that slot into matching holes in the main plates. This entire inner section is sandwiched between two large plywood plates shaped like an hourglass. The top plate has numerous circular cutouts for buttons, while the bottom plate is mostly solid. Two small metal screws are positioned above the top plate to show where it fastens into the assembly.

g.title: Game Controller Inventory List
g.desc: This image lists the parts needed for the project: one 'Top' and one 'Bottom' piece (flat laser-cut plywood), two 1/2 inch brass screws, two 'Large Spacers' and two 'Small Spacers' (small wooden rings of varying sizes), one flexible 'Jumper Wire' with plastic tips, and one 'Custom PCBA' (a flat circuit board with electronic components).

g.title: Controller Component Identification and Layout
g.desc: This component guide displays the parts in the kit. There are two large plywood pieces shaped like a game controller; the 'Top' piece has eight small rectangular cutouts, and the 'Bottom' piece has six cutouts with a central pill-shaped slot near the bottom edge. Also included is a large, solid rectangular circuit board (PCBA) and a flat ribbon of several parallel jumper wires with a plastic connector at one end. You will find two pairs of thin wooden strips: two 'Large Spacers' (long with tabs on the ends) and two 'Small Spacers' (shorter with tabs). Finally, identify two small 1/2-inch brass screws.

g.title: Small Spacer component identification
g.desc: This component is a small cross-shaped piece of 5mm laser-cut plywood. It consists of a horizontal rectangular body with a small square tab protruding from the center of both the top and bottom edges. You should find two of these identical spacers in your kit.

g.title: Large Spacer Wood Component
g.desc: Find two identical long, thin plywood pieces. One end has a wide rectangular block near the tip. The long middle section is a narrow beam. The opposite end finishes with a cross-like shape, featuring two small tabs that stick out from the top and bottom edges.

g.title: Controller Top Wooden Plate
g.desc: A large, flat, 3mm thick wooden piece shaped like a game controller with two rounded handles. Each side has four cutouts for identification: two vertical rectangular slots (top and bottom), a small square slot in the middle, and a small circular hole just below the square.

g.title: Long Rectangular PCBA Module
g.desc: Locate a single, long and narrow rectangular circuit board with smooth, rounded corners. As a Printed Circuit Board Assembly (PCBA), it will feel rigid and have various small electronic components soldered to its surface. This module is significantly longer than it is wide.

g.title: Five-Conductor Ribbon Jumper Wire
g.desc: This component is a single bundle of five thin, flexible wires joined together side-by-side to form a flat ribbon. On one end, all five wires are secured into a single wide, rectangular plastic connector. On the other end, the wires separate into five individual single-pin connectors. You can identify this part by feeling the flat, ribbed texture of the wires and finding the contrast between the one large solid connector at one end and the five small, separate connector tips at the other.

g.title: Large Symmetrical Bottom Plate
g.desc: A single 3mm thick laser-cut wooden piece shaped like a wide, rounded bowtie or hourglass. It features several distinct tactile markers for orientation: a horizontal capsule-shaped slot is located in the lower center. Near the two upper rounded lobes are tall vertical rectangular slots. Near the two lower lobes are small square slots. On both the left and right sides, between the top and bottom slots, is a small circular hole positioned just above another small square slot.

g.title: Two 1/2 Inch Brass Screws
g.desc: Locate two small brass screws. Each screw has a round head with a single slot and a threaded shaft. You can identify these by measuring the shaft length, which is exactly one-half inch from the flat underside of the head to the tip.
